expectations.text: Contains a concrete analogy using familiar real-world concepts
expectations.evidence: Pokemon card trading notebook analogy — kids keeping copies and catching cheaters.

expectations.text: Explicitly states where the analogy breaks down
expectations.evidence: No mention anywhere of where the analogy stops working or diverges from reality.

expectations.text: Includes a visual (tldraw, Mermaid, or structured)
expectations.evidence: No visual of any kind produced. Pure text only.

expectations.text: The visual teaches something the text alone does not

expectations.evidence: Has sections (What is / Why care) but no explicit one-liner, and jumps from analogy directly to reasons without a clear layered build.

expectations.text: Includes curated external links with descriptions
expectations.evidence: No external links or resources included.

expectations.text: Technical terms introduced only after being tied to familiar concept
expectations.evidence: Terms like 'smart contracts' introduced with concrete example (sports bet). 'Blocks' explained as pages in notebook.

expectations.text: No single section introduces more than 4 new concepts
expectations.evidence: Each numbered reason section focuses on one concept.
